ambiguities.requirement: The system shall return to the appropriate page.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: In the example in Figure 20.11, you will not only return to either the index or the item display
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow users to return to either the index or the item display.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall return to the correct type of index, either alphabetical or by category.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is not supported by the source text. The original text mentions returning to 'the right type of index', which could be interpreted as a single type, not multiple types.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall return to the appropriate page.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is partially supported by the source text. However, it does not specify what constitutes an 'appropriate' page.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall return to either the index or the item display.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous because it does not clarify which one should be returned to first, and what happens if both are required.
